LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF CAVALRY PORTFOLIO SERVICES, LLC'S MOTION FOR ASSIGNMENT ORDER IS DENIED. THERE IS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THE REQUESTED RELIEF. IN PARTICULAR, THERE IS NO COMPETENT EVIDENCE ABOUT THE NATURE OF ANY EARNINGS, COMMISSIONS AND OTHER MONIES DUE TO THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR FROM THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn For Assignment Order; Memo Of P & A And Pos
Off Calendar May-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF CAVALRY PORTFOLIO SERVICES, LLC'S MOTION For Assignment Order IS
Denied. There is insufficient evidence to support the requested relief. In particular, there is no competent evidence about the nature of any earnings, commissions and other monies due to the judgment debtor from the California Department of Social Services. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)
